---
name: frontend-design
description: "Design and implement polished frontend UI. Use when user asks to beautify pages, create landing/dashboard sections, improve visual hierarchy, refine typography/colors/spacing, or add tasteful motion."
---

# Frontend Design

## When to use
- Build or improve static HTML/CSS UI in `public/`.
- Refresh layout and styling while preserving existing behavior.
- Add UI sections with strong readability and clear call-to-action.

## Procedure
1. Pick one clear visual direction that fits the feature and audience.
2. Implement production-ready markup and styles (not mock-only code).
3. Keep accessibility and responsiveness intact.
4. Reuse project patterns; avoid unnecessary UI dependencies.

## Design guardrails
- Prefer cohesive spacing and typography over flashy but inconsistent effects.
- Use animation only where it improves clarity or feedback.
